report_name: Chubb Report: Average US Data Breach Cost Soars to $10.2 Million, Driven by AI Weaponization and Supply Chain Failures

threat_actors.name: Unspecified Cybercriminals
threat_actors.description: A generic representation of the cybercriminals whose activities and trends are analyzed in the Chubb 2026 Cyber Claims Report. This includes actors leveraging AI, conducting supply chain attacks, and using ransomware.

attack_patterns.name: Phishing
attack_patterns.description: Identified in the report as a major attack vector, accounting for over 41% of ransomware incidents.

attack_patterns.name: Supply Chain Compromise
attack_patterns.description: Identified as a key driver for rising data breach costs and considered the top cyber challenge by 65% of large companies due to vulnerabilities in third-party software.

attack_patterns.name: Generate Deepfakes
attack_patterns.description: A specific application of hostile AI mentioned in the report, used for sophisticated deepfake-based social engineering attacks.

attack_patterns.name: Polymorphic Code
attack_patterns.description: A capability of hostile AI mentioned in the report, used to create self-rewriting malware that is more evasive to traditional defenses.

attack_patterns.name: Data Encrypted for Impact
attack_patterns.description: The core technique used in ransomware attacks, such as the one against Jaguar Land Rover, which leads to business disruption and significant financial costs.

tools.name: Artificial Intelligence
tools.description: Weaponized by cybercriminals to create self-rewriting malware and conduct sophisticated deepfake-based social engineering attacks, as noted in the Chubb report.

identities.name: Jaguar Land Rover
identities.description: Cited as a case study for a major ransomware attack that caused significant economic loss to the wider UK economy due to manufacturing halts and supply chain disruption.

identities.name: Large Enterprises
identities.description: Identified as facing the highest breach costs and being the primary focus of major litigation following data breaches.

identities.name: Technology and Software Companies
identities.description: Identified as being both targets and vectors for software supply chain attacks.

identities.name: Critical Infrastructure
identities.description: Described as high-value targets for AI-driven attacks, facing massive operational and economic fallout from disruptions.
